description: Shared Sense360 firmware / entity framework contract: the stable capability registry, the module-status and device-health value contracts, and the per-config-string compile-time capability declarations that products/bundles/*.yaml implement via s360_* substitutions and packages/base/device_framework.yaml. Docs describe; this file decides. Validated by tests/test_core_framework.py.
guardrails: All capability and module-status values in this contract are compile-time composition facts only: no runtime hardware autodetection is performed or claimed by the framework.
Module status 'Included' is compile-time presence, never a health, availability, bench or hardware claim.

The reserved runtime status values (Initialising / Available / Degraded / Unavailable / Fault) may only be introduced by a module-specific PR that wires a real supported runtime signal. Presence is the first wired module (PRESENCE-FRAMEWORK-001), RoomIQ is the second (ROOMIQ-FRAMEWORK-001) and AirIQ is the third (AIRIQ-FRAMEWORK-001) — see module_runtime_status; all other module slots still emit compile-time values only.
This contract changes no release declaration: config/webflash-builds.json rows, channels, versions, artifact names, product lifecycle and commercial state are untouched.
Compile-only skeletons (products/compile-only/) and the provisioning bench harness (tests/bench/) never include the framework package, so they cannot be mistaken for customer products.
Firmware-build proof only: nothing in this contract claims hardware, bench, compliance, safety or commercial-availability proof.

Ceiling-POE-FanPWM is the one declared framework gap (framework_included: false): its bundle is pinned package-identical to the native full-compile-validated skeleton, so the framework include is deferred until that lane re-records. All other configs compose the framework exactly once.

core.description: Sense360 Core hub board (ESP32-S3). Present in every composition; owns WiFi/API/OTA base infrastructure.

power_poe.description: PoE power composition: the S360-410 PoE PSU package is compiled in (power-source, supply-voltage and PoE-status entities).

power_usb.description: USB power composition: the USB power package (packages/hardware/power_usb.yaml) is compiled in (power-source and supply-voltage entities); no PSU module package is composed.

roomiq.description: RoomIQ environmental-service firmware compiled from the RoomIQ climate half (SHT4x temperature/humidity + VEML7700 ambient light) on the S360-200 board, with the ROOMIQ-FRAMEWORK-001 customer experience (calibrated Temperature/Humidity/Illuminance, Comfort, Environment State, Brightness and the canonical darkness service consumed by LED; packages/features/roomiq_framework.yaml). Describes compiled firmware only, not the board BOM (the board's LTR-303ALS light-sensor identity is not compiled and remains a pending bench reconciliation).

airiq.description: AirIQ air-quality firmware (SCD4x CO2, SGP4x VOC/NOx, SPS30 particulate, BMP3xx pressure) on the S360-210 board, with the AIRIQ-FRAMEWORK-001 customer experience (canonical CO2 / VOC / NOx / PM2.5, one worst-pollutant Air Quality headline, one Recommendation and the AirIQ module runtime status; packages/features/airiq_framework.yaml). Describes compiled firmware only. The MICS-4514 gas stage exists on the hardware but is not compiled in any current configuration and is NOT part of this capability (diagnostic-only engine channels exist pending ENTITY-FILL-210-MICS-001 calibration evidence).

ventiq.description: VentIQ bathroom air-quality firmware (SGP4x VOC/NOx, SHT4x temperature/humidity, BMP3xx pressure) on the S360-211 board. Describes compiled firmware only.

presence.description: Tri-sensor Presence firmware on the S360-200 board (PRESENCE-FRAMEWORK-001): HLK-LD2450 mmWave occupancy/target tracking on the radar UART, the PIR motion input (schematic IO15) and the SEN0609 static-presence digital output (schematic IO6), fused into one customer occupancy capability by packages/features/presence_framework.yaml. Describes compiled firmware only; no hardware/bench proof is claimed.

led.description: S360-300 LED ring firmware: WS2812 RMT LED-strip driver with the LED-FRAMEWORK-001 customer experience (Room Light, Night Mode, status/identify overlays; packages/features/led_framework.yaml). Describes compiled firmware only; the one-way WS2812B data line carries no runtime health signal, so LED module status stays compile-time.

fan_relay.description: S360-310 on/off relay fan driver (experimental lane).

fan_pwm.description: S360-311 12V PWM fan driver with tach feedback.

fan_triac.description: S360-320 TRIAC phase dimmer (experimental self-build mains lane; never stable / buyable).

values.Not included: The module's packages are not part of this configuration's compiled composition. This is the normal state for an optional module that was not ordered — it is never a fault.
values.Included: Compile-time presence only: the module's packages are compiled into this firmware. It does not mean the module was detected, is healthy, or is available — runtime health signals arrive with the module-specific PRs.

reserved_values.Initialising: Reserved: module is present and inside its startup warm-up window; must not be reported as Unavailable during warm-up.
reserved_values.Available: Reserved: module is present and a real supported runtime signal (successful sensor update, component status, or communication success) confirms it is working. Never derived from compilation alone.
reserved_values.Degraded: Reserved: module responds but part of its function is impaired (e.g. one of several sensors failing or stale). Must not mark unrelated modules unavailable.
reserved_values.Unavailable: Reserved: module is expected (compiled in) but a real runtime signal shows it is not responding after warm-up.
reserved_values.Fault: Reserved: module reports an explicit error condition. Never used for a module that is simply absent.

presence.available_definition: Available means STRICTLY: the verifiable Presence transport (the LD2450 UART, whose frame-driven updates are the only real health signal) is fresh after warm-up and the fusion service is operational. It is service availability, NOT full tri-sensor hardware health: the PIR and SEN0609 GPIO channels remain unverifiable and are never claimed healthy. The diagnostic entity s360_presence_verification_limits ('Presence Sensor Verification') states this coverage limit on-device.
signals: LD2450 frame-driven update timestamps (target-count sensor on_value callback on the radar UART) provide real data-freshness evidence; no frame past the stale window means UNKNOWN, never clear.
Per-sensor startup windows (PIR / LD2450 / SEN0609) gate Initialising; a real LD2450 frame ends its initialisation early.
Explicit module-fault input is reserved: no composed sensor exposes a supported fault signal today, so Fault is never emitted from ordinary stale data.
presence.notes: Honesty limits: the PIR and SEN0609 digital-output channels are bare GPIO levels and cannot prove communication health — a permanently low line may be a healthy sensor in an empty room or a disconnected one, so these channels never qualify the module as fully Available on their own and never drive it Unavailable. Available therefore attests the verifiable (UART) sensor set only; runtime status never claims hardware/bench proof. Expected-sensor membership is configuration-driven (presence_*_expected substitutions): an intentionally absent sensor in a future composition is never a fault.

roomiq.available_definition: Available means STRICTLY: every required environmental channel (temperature, humidity, illuminance) delivered a valid value-update inside its stale window — real data-freshness evidence from the I2C sensor update callbacks, evaluated by the shared engine include/sense360/roomiq_engine.h. It is data-service availability, NOT sensor accuracy, calibration correctness or hardware health, and it does not resolve the ambient-light part identity (catalog/BOM LTR-303ALS-01 vs compiled VEML7700, pending bench). The diagnostic entity s360_roomiq_sensor_verification ('RoomIQ Sensor Verification') states this coverage limit on-device.
signals: Per-channel valid (non-NaN) value-update timestamps for temperature, humidity and illuminance provide real data-freshness evidence; a channel past its stale window is unusable and its values go unknown, never frozen.
Per-channel warm-up windows gate Initialising; the first valid sample ends a channel's initialisation early. Startup is never reported as a fault or an outage.
Degraded means at least one channel is fresh while another is stale/missing (e.g. illuminance failed but the SHT4x climate path still updates — Comfort stays available, Brightness goes unavailable).
Explicit module-fault input is reserved: no composed sensor exposes a supported fault signal today, so Fault is never emitted from ordinary stale data.
roomiq.notes: Honesty limits: temperature and humidity share one physical sensor (SHT4x @ 0x44), so their shared failure appears as both channels going stale together; the engine still tracks them independently. Freshness attests data delivery only — all comfort/brightness thresholds are provisional heuristics and no hardware/bench proof is claimed. The customer-facing entity names never carry a sensor model name while the light-sensor identity stays unresolved.

airiq.available_definition: Available means STRICTLY: every EXPECTED pollutant channel of this composition (CO2, VOC, NOx, PM2.5 by default — configuration-driven via the airiq_expected_* substitutions, never an invented Base/Pro axis) delivered a valid value-update inside its per-sensor stale window — real data-freshness evidence from the I2C sensor update callbacks, evaluated by the shared engine include/sense360/airiq_engine.h. It is data-service availability, NOT sensor accuracy, calibration correctness or hardware health, and it does not resolve the S360-210 pressure part identity (compiled BMP390 @0x77 vs a BOM listing no pressure part — pressure is therefore excluded from health while the reconciliation is open). The diagnostic entity s360_airiq_sensor_verification ('AirIQ Sensor Verification') states this coverage limit on-device.
signals: Per-channel valid value-update timestamps for CO2, VOC, NOx and PM2.5 provide real data-freshness evidence; a channel past its stale window is unusable and its values go unknown, never frozen — stale data never counts as good air.

Independent per-sensor warm-up windows gate Initialising (SCD41/SPS30 60 s, SGP41 120 s — provisional data-availability windows, never accuracy claims); the first valid sample ends a channel's initialisation early. Startup is never reported as a fault or an outage.

Degraded means at least one EXPECTED channel is stale/missing while another stays fresh (e.g. the connector-attached SPS30 goes silent but the SCD41/SGP41 path still updates — an honest partial Air Quality headline remains).
Explicit module-fault input is reserved: no composed sensor exposes a supported fault signal today, so Fault is never emitted from ordinary stale data.
airiq.notes: Honesty limits: the SPS30 is connector-attached but compiled as part of the standard stack (and named in the HW-AIRIQ-WAIVER-2026-06 stable waiver), so it is expected by default; the MICS-4514 + STM8 gas stage and the SFA40 formaldehyde stage are NOT compiled (interfaces unverified — ENTITY-FILL-210-MICS-001 / ENTITY-FILL-210-HCHO-001) and never affect health; the compiled BMP390 pressure path is freshness-tracked but excluded from health and from Air Quality severity while its BOM identity conflict is unresolved. Formaldehyde/ozone are engine contract slots with expected=false in every current composition. Runtime status never claims hardware/bench proof; an intentionally absent optional sensor is never a fault.

values.Starting: Device is inside the boot warm-up window (s360_health_warmup_seconds, default 120 s). Startup must not be reported as a fault.
values.Running: Base firmware completed its startup window and no framework-level fault signal exists. This states nothing about modules or sensors: no module runtime-health signal is connected yet, and no physical sensor has been verified.

reserved_values.Healthy: Reserved: every included module reports real runtime health (module-specific PRs). Never derived from the warm-up window or from compilation alone.
reserved_values.Degraded: Reserved: at least one included module is Degraded or Unavailable while the rest of the device works.
reserved_values.Fault: Reserved: a critical module or the device itself reports an explicit fault.
reserved_values.Safe mode: Reserved: the device booted into ESPHome safe mode / recovery. Requires a supported runtime signal before it can be reported.

reserved_inputs: stale-sensor-data detection (distinguished from a missing module)

Ceiling-POE-FanPWM.deferral_reason: Deferred: products/bundles/ceiling-poe-fanpwm.yaml must compose the IDENTICAL package set as the native full-compile-validated compile-only skeleton products/compile-only/ceiling-poe-fanpwm-native.yaml (S360-311-NATIVE-FANPWM-COMPILE-001, tests/test_pwm_product_readiness.py). Adding the framework package would break that identity and silently invalidate the recorded native full-compile evidence. The framework include lands for this config in a follow-up once the native compile-only lane re-records a framework-bearing composition.
